label: Cloudflare
description: Manage Cloudflare DNS, Workers, R2, KV, Pages, and more via the Cloudflare API.

form.title: Connect Cloudflare
form.subtitle: Authorize Anton to manage your Cloudflare zones, Workers, R2 buckets, and other resources.

methods.label: API Token
methods.description: Paste a scoped API token from My Profile → API Tokens. Recommended — tokens are revocable, minimally scoped, and far safer than a Global API Key.
methods.how_to:
## Create a scoped Cloudflare API Token

API Tokens are the modern, preferred way to authenticate with Cloudflare. You control exactly which resources and permissions are granted.

### Steps

1. Log in to the [Cloudflare dashboard](https://dash.cloudflare.com).
2. Click your profile icon (top-right) → **My Profile**.
3. Select the **API Tokens** tab → **Create Token**.
4. Choose **Create Custom Token** (or start from a template like *Edit zone DNS*).
5. Under **Permissions**, add the resource types you need Anton to access — for example:
   - Zone / DNS / Edit
   - Account / Workers Scripts / Edit
   - Account / R2 Storage / Edit
6. Under **Account Resources**, pick **All accounts** or scope to a specific account.
7. Optionally restrict by **IP filtering** or set a **TTL**.
8. Click **Continue to summary → Create Token**.
9. Copy the token — it is shown **only once**. Paste it into the **API Token** field below.
10. Find your **Account ID** on the right-hand sidebar of the Cloudflare dashboard home page (or any zone overview page) and paste it below.

### Notes

- If the token is lost, generate a new one — they cannot be retrieved after creation.
- Revoke tokens any time from **My Profile → API Tokens → Revoke**.
- Most Account-level resources (Workers, R2, KV) require the Account ID; Zone-level resources (DNS) require a Zone ID, which you can add as an optional field per-request.

fields.name: api_token
fields.label: API Token
fields.description: Scoped API token from My Profile → API Tokens. Shown only once at creation.

fields.name: account_id
fields.label: Account ID
fields.description: Found on the right-hand sidebar of your Cloudflare dashboard home or any zone page.

methods.label: Global API Key
methods.description: Use your account-wide Global API Key and email. Not recommended — the Global API Key has full account access and cannot be scoped. Prefer an API Token where possible.
methods.how_to:
## Use the Global API Key

The Global API Key grants unrestricted access to your entire Cloudflare account. Use it only if a scoped API Token is not available for your use-case.

### Steps

1. Log in to the [Cloudflare dashboard](https://dash.cloudflare.com).
2. Click your profile icon (top-right) → **My Profile**.
3. Select the **API Tokens** tab.
4. Scroll down to the **Global API Key** section and click **View**.
5. Complete any 2FA prompt, then copy the key.
6. Paste the key into the **Global API Key** field below, and enter the **Email** associated with your Cloudflare account.
7. Find your **Account ID** on the dashboard sidebar and paste it below.

### Notes

- The Global API Key never expires and cannot be scoped — treat it like a root password.
- If you suspect exposure, rotate it immediately from the same page.
- Prefer a scoped **API Token** for all new integrations.

fields.name: email
fields.label: Cloudflare Email
fields.description: The email address associated with your Cloudflare account.

fields.name: global_api_key
fields.label: Global API Key
fields.description: From My Profile → API Tokens → Global API Key → View. Full account access — use with caution.

fields.name: account_id
fields.label: Account ID
fields.description: Found on the right-hand sidebar of your Cloudflare dashboard home or any zone page.
